---
title: "4.12 2D Array Traversals"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## 2D Array Traversals

### Traversing a 2D array requires nested loops

This topic extends 1D traversal logic into row-and-column structures.

::: {.tight-list}
- a 2D traversal visits all elements or a defined sequence of elements
- because there are rows and columns, one loop is not enough
- nested loops are the standard pattern
:::

## Nested Loops

### One loop controls rows and one loop controls columns

The retained structure is:

```java
for (int row = 0; row < arr.length; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col < arr[0].length; col++)
    {
        // use arr[row][col]
    }
}
```

Students should connect the total number of iterations to rows times columns.

## Row-Major and Column-Major Traversals

### Traversal order depends on which loop is outside

Retained distinction:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">row-major</span>: move across a row before going to the next row
- <span class="term">column-major</span>: move down a column before going to the next column
- the loop order determines the traversal order
:::

This is a frequent AP tracing target.

## Enhanced For-Each Loop for 2D Arrays

### The outer loop visits rows, and the inner loop visits elements in each row

The textbook keeps one exact mental model:

::: {.tight-list}
- the outer enhanced `for` variable must be a 1D array type
- the inner enhanced `for` variable must be the element type
- enhanced `for` is good for reading all values
- assigning to the loop variable does not change stored primitive values
:::

## 2D Arrays of Objects

### Traversal can also manipulate object state

![A pixel inside an image grid](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-12-2d-array-traversals/pixel.jpg){fig-align="center" width="44%"}

When a 2D array stores object references, traversal can call methods on those objects.

::: {.tight-list}
- a picture can be modeled as a 2D array of `Pixel` objects
- nested traversal can visit every pixel
- method calls can change object attributes such as RGB values
:::

## Picture Lab Pattern

### 2D traversal becomes a concrete image-processing workflow

![Picture Lab source image](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-12-2d-array-traversals/arch.jpg){fig-align="center" width="58%"}

The retained teaching idea is:

::: {.tight-list}
- visit every pixel in the picture
- read red, green, and blue values
- modify one or more color channels
- observe the transformed image
:::

This makes nested loops concrete instead of abstract.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- write and trace nested loops for full 2D traversal
- distinguish row-major and column-major order
- explain the variable types in nested enhanced `for` loops
- explain why enhanced `for` does not modify primitive values directly
- traverse 2D arrays of objects and call mutator methods
- <span class="term">4.12.5 Coding Challenge: Picture Lab</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain why 2D traversal needs nested loops
- distinguish row-major from column-major traversal
- describe the outer and inner variable types in a nested enhanced `for` loop
- explain why enhanced `for` cannot directly replace primitive 2D array values
- explain how a 2D array of objects can still be modified through method calls
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
